author: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy
book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.

Hyle, Which Was Called by the Ancients the Formless Material of Things, is Not an Evil.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Hyle, Which Was Called by the Ancients the Formless Material of Things, is Not an Evil.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For neither is that material, which the ancients called Hyle, to be called an evil.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: I do not say that which Manichæus with most senseless vanity, not knowing what he says, denominates Hyle, namely, the former of corporeal beings; whence it is rightly said to him, that he introduces another god.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For nobody can form and create corporeal beings but God alone; for neither are they created unless there subsist with them measure, form, and order, which I think that now even they themselves confess to be good things, and things that cannot be except from God.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: But by Hyle I mean a certain material absolutely formless and without quality, whence those qualities that we perceive are formed, as the ancients said.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For hence also wood is called in Greek ὕλη, because it is adapted to workmen, not that itself may make anything, but that it is the material of which something may be made.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Nor is that Hyle, therefore, to be called an evil which cannot be perceived through any appearance, but can scarcely be thought of through any sort of privation of appearance.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For this has also a capacity of forms; for if it cannot receive the form imposed by the workman, neither assuredly may it be called material.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Hence if form is some good, whence those who excel in it are called beautiful, as from appearance they are called handsome, even the capacity of form is undoubtedly something good.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: As because wisdom is a good, no one doubts that to be capable of wisdom is a good.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: And because every good is from God, no one ought to doubt that even matter, if there is any, has its existence from God alone.
